tips: Combine simple reactants into a single, more complex product.
Check charge balance when combining metal and nonmetal.
A single reactant breaks down into multiple products.
An element reacts with a compound, replacing one element.
Use the activity series to check if the reaction will actually occur.
Two compounds exchange ions to form two new compounds.
Usually forms a precipitate, gas, or weak electrolyte (like water).
A hydrocarbon reacts with oxygen gas.
Products are always carbon dioxide and water vapor (for complete combustion).
